behaviors.description: The content passed to summary generation includes data from metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (three collectors -> aggregation -> summary input), not a single-function algorithm. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network is required to prove this behavior; in-process fakes for the three sources are sufficient. LLM-DEP: assertion is on pre-LLM payload, not model judgment. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered but rejected because behavior text is specifically about "content passed to the summary-generation step," which is verifiable before real external I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/aggregation modules with three stub source adapters returning distinct fixtures; execute one report run and assert the exact payload handed to the summary step contains all three sections with correct mapping.

behaviors.description: If one source is unavailable, report generation continues and includes a 'data unavailable' note for that section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module resilience/state propagation (collector error -> section annotation -> final report), so Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: source unavailability can be reproduced by adapter exceptions/timeouts in-process. LLM-DEP: correctness is fallback behavior, not model quality. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered but rejected because behavior text requires graceful degradation logic, which is testable via controlled adapter failure without real network.
behaviors.test_description: Run report assembly with two normal source adapters and one failing adapter; assert process does not abort, output contains the failed section marked 'data unavailable', and other sections are still populated.

behaviors.description: Narrative summary accurately reflects key values in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on actual summary content quality/faithfulness, which varies with real model behavior and cannot be validated by deterministic stubs. MOCK-HIDE: mocking summary generation would always hide hallucination/omission failures. MULTI-STEP: single model decision stage, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was rejected because behavior text is about "accurately represents" semantic output quality, not API wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative report inputs with known key metrics/incidents/deploy counts into the real summary model; evaluate whether generated narrative preserves those key facts (with acceptance rubric and repeated runs to account for variability).

behaviors.description: End-to-end report generation and email delivery reaches configured recipient inbox.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: requires real HTTP/API calls, real email transport, and inbox delivery behavior across process/network boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: mocked mail/API layers can pass while real auth, transport, MIME, or delivery issues fail. LLM-DEP: although a summary step may exist, this behavior's pass criterion is delivery/inbox arrival, so model output quality is not the asserted boundary. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered and rejected because behavior text is "reaches ... inbox correctly," i.e., infrastructure delivery outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script in a production-like environment against real internal endpoints (or realistic staging equivalents) and real mail delivery path to a test inbox; assert message arrival, recipient correctness, and expected report attachment/body presence.

behaviors.description: If email service is unreachable, save generated report locally and retry delivery on next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: failure mode includes real delivery-service reachability plus persistence across runs; network/socket and run-to-run behavior are common real-world breakpoints. MOCK-HIDE: pure mocks can miss true unreachable conditions and retry timing/state interactions. MULTI-STEP: this is retry across runs but not multiple agentic decisions. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text explicitly includes "email delivery service is unreachable" and "retried on the next scheduled run," both tied to real infra/runtime boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Run once with delivery endpoint intentionally unreachable (e.g., blocked SMTP/API host) and verify report is written to local fallback path; trigger next scheduled execution with service restored and verify previously saved report is retried and successfully delivered.
